title: Whatever happened to the OTHER concurrency
display_title: Whatever happened to the OTHER concurrency
wikitext:
[]: Whatever happened to the OTHER concurrency?             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 30, 2014 4:52 pm Something odd occurred to me just now and I'd like to see what people think about it, however I want to say upfront that I don't actually endorse this idea. It's... well, you'll see. First, some background for our newer friends. Eva has two different endings: episodes 25 and 26 as originally boadcast, also known as EoTV (End of TV) episodes 25' and 26' as released in theatres as End of Evangelion (EoE), along with other additional materials. Deciding what this means has caused a lot of consternation and arguments over the years. Generally the various ideas have boiled down to a few answers: The endings stand as different outcomes (no concurrency) Both tell the same story from different narrative frameworks (weak concurrency) Both happen at different moments in time (strong concurrency) I happen to prefer option #1, but what I want to focus on today is #3. Specifically, the fact that in every instance I can recall this has been accomplished by slotting in the events of EoTV as happening between scenes of EoE and ending with the famous beach scene. What I really want to know is this: Why haven't I ever seen an argument for EoE happening within EoTV? For example, couldn't everything that happens after Shinji's decision to return to the real world actually still be within instrumentality, specifically the creation of his unstable isolated 'world he wished for' that the last episode of the series was trying to save him from? Again, I'm not arguing in favor of this being true (though if anyone is interested in doing so, feel free), but rather I'm wondering what it is about EoE that leads people to feel like it's unquestionably the 'dominant' half of the equation when trying to make it all work together.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Aug 30, 2014 5:20 pm It could be because the same audience who would argue for EoTV as the dominant half prefers #1 anyway. Yourself, for example. Anyone who hates EoE enough to prefer that it just not exist and that EoTV is the "true", "good" end has no incentive to argue in favor of any form of concurrency. That's my best attempt.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Aug 30, 2014 5:29 pm I think its simpler than that -- EoTV is (a handful of glimpses aside) all in the interior world, whereas EoE is rooted in the objective world, complete with military operations and giant robot battles that serve to tie up the loose plot threads.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 30, 2014 9:38 pm I guess that makes as much sense as anything. Thought this might stir up some discussion, but oh well.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Aug 30, 2014 10:02 pm What is the difference between "EoE in EoTV" and "EoTV in EoE"? I don't see how it's even remotely possible to solve the biggest concurrency hurdle, i.e. the tone and nature of the endings. Unles EoTV cuts off right before the beach which is kind of a douche move.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 30, 2014 10:15 pm The biggest difference would probably be which one gets the final word on instrumentality, which necessarily changes the entire tone of the combined work. It would mean nobody is dead, Yui doesn't leave, Gendo was right all along and all that unpleasantness at the end was the form Shinji's isolated, self-destructive 'world he wished for' takes because of his belief that he deserves to be abandoned. I agree tone whiplash would be a hell of a problem, but then I think the same problem applies to the more common attempt at concurrency as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Aug 30, 2014 10:15 pm EoTV "Congratulations!" would be thought to happen after EoE's "I want to see them again, because I believe my feelings at that time were real." in terms of tone matching (Instrumentality ending) for weak or strong concurrency.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 30, 2014 10:21 pm How does the rest fit in again? It's been a long while since I've considered that combined film project of yours. Can you summarize the arrangement briefly? perhaps something like: [EoTV stuff happens] (EoE stuff happens) [EoTV again] (etc...)

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Aug 30, 2014 10:26 pm The entire concurrency debate is an example of how not to approach a work of fiction. Evangelion isn't a documentary. None of it actually happened, so trying to work out how two different versions of the ending happened at the same "time" or devising a relationship is largely unnecessary. Parts of EoE may have been born as content that was unproduced for the TV ending for whatever reason, but there's not a chance in hell Congratulations and How Disgusting are meant to depict the same event and mashing them together, as you said, makes no sense. Either Shinji rediscovered his will to live and EoTV cuts off right before a blasted hellscape or EoTV takes place in Shinji's head while his eyes go all vacant and he's flipped his cookie. If all of the beach scene is part of Instrumentality and another alternate reality Shinji created to punish and isolate himself before his big revelation in EoTV, that makes EoE a shaggy dog story. Rather the real question is, why the tonal whiplash? Why end on damn near a 1980's sitcom freeze frame and then turn around and change it to an expressionist hellscape?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Aug 30, 2014 10:49 pm Chuckman: Huh?? Sailor Star Dust wrote: after EoE's "I want to see them again, because I believe my feelings at that time were real."

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Aug 30, 2014 11:00 pm The endings are tonally incompatible. No matter how you order it, pushing one into the other makes one half meaningless.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 30, 2014 11:01 pm Chuckman , I agree... but the point of the thread is asking why, if for the sake of argument we assume concurrency is a thing, is it always arranged this way and not another? SSD , I was really hoping for some sort of planning documentation. Surely you guys didn't just edit it all together on the fly, right? I really don't want to have to watch the whole huge thing just to know how it's put together.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Aug 31, 2014 12:17 am Chuckman wrote: The endings are tonally incompatible. No matter how you order it, pushing one into the other makes one half meaningless.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Aug 31, 2014 2:41 am Guy Nacks wrote: Life is tonally incompatible. You start out as your father's orgasm and end by shitting your own pants.

[]:             Quote Post by Ispellnogood » Sun Aug 31, 2014 7:41 am 1.The endings stand as different outcomes (no concurrency)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 31, 2014 11:31 am NemZ: If you want planning, check out my first post in that thread! ;) I started the whole idea in 2007 or so, long before anybody (Brik, Sommen, etc) put footage together.

[]:             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Wed Sep 03, 2014 10:27 am Might be a bit off topic, but the way I've always seen it is the moment in EoE where Shinji rejects instrumentality is concurrent with the "Congratulations!" scene in EoTV. The swelling music and visuals all have a triumphant feel to them. Then we return from EoTV with Shinji into the real world of EoE, and there's Rei's giant bisected head and a dead earth. Hence why it's called 'One More Final', because it takes place chronologically after EoTV.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Sep 03, 2014 11:49 am Th3Marauder wrote: Might be a bit off topic, but the way I've always seen it is the moment in EoE where Shinji rejects instrumentality is concurrent with the "Congratulations!" scene in EoTV. The swelling music and visuals all have a triumphant feel to them. Then we return from EoTV with Shinji into the real world of EoE, and there's Rei's giant bisected head and a dead earth. Hence why it's called 'One More Final', because it takes place chronologically after EoTV.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Sep 03, 2014 12:14 pm Th3Marauder wrote: Might be a bit off topic, but the way I've always seen it is the moment in EoE where Shinji rejects instrumentality is concurrent with the "Congratulations!" scene in EoTV. The swelling music and visuals all have a triumphant feel to them. Then we return from EoTV with Shinji into the real world of EoE, and there's Rei's giant bisected head and a dead earth. Hence why it's called 'One More Final', because it takes place chronologically after EoTV.
